Configurable registration, a dedicated TURN domain/port, and automatic federation discovery (`.well-known`). Resolves issue #5.

## Added

- **`ENABLE_REGISTRATION`** (default `false`). When `true`, open self-service signup is enabled and Element shows its **Create Account** button (`UIFeature.registration`). It stays off by default — only enable it behind a CAPTCHA or on a trusted network, or you will get spam signups. (Synapse refuses to start with open registration and no verification, so the container also sets `enable_registration_without_verification` to match the toggle.)
- **`TURN_DOMAIN`** (default: `SERVER_NAME`) and **`TURN_PORT`** (default `3478`). Route TURN (voice/video) through a dedicated subdomain and/or a remapped port — e.g. to take coturn around a reverse proxy. The `turn_uris` Synapse advertises now follow these instead of being hard-coded to `SERVER_NAME:3478`.

## Fixed

- **Federation discovery now works out of the box.** The image previously claimed to serve `/.well-known/matrix/server` and `/.well-known/matrix/client` on port 8080, but nothing actually served them, so federation returned 404 unless you hand-wrote the JSON in your reverse proxy. Synapse now serves both itself via `serve_server_wellknown` + `public_baseurl`, delegating incoming federation to port **443** — so as long as `https://SERVER_NAME/` is routed to Synapse (the standard reverse-proxy setup), federation works with **no extra proxy rules**. Any manual `/.well-known/matrix/*` locations can be removed.

## Changed

- README section 6 (Enabling Federation) rewritten: no manual `.well-known` proxy locations are needed anymore.

Thanks to @i-byrana (issue #5) for the detailed report and the suggested fixes.
